options.content:
## CelestiaOps — LAMOST Stellar Spectroscopy
Spectroscopic parameters for exoplanet host stars observed by the **LAMOST** (Large Sky Area Multi-Object Fiber Spectroscopic Telescope) DR5 survey. Data sourced from VizieR TAP (V/164/stellar5), cone-searched within 5 arcsec of each NASA exoplanet host star. LAMOST coverage: FGK stars in the northern sky (dec > −10°), V ≈10–17.8 mag. **Exoplanet context** panels at the bottom draw from the full NASA Exoplanet Archive for cross-reference.

panels.description: Total number of LAMOST spectroscopic observations matched to NASA exoplanet host stars. Each host star may have up to 5 observations ranked by g-band SNR.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM lamost_observations

panels.title: LAMOST Observations

panels.description: Number of distinct exoplanet host stars with at least one LAMOST spectroscopic match.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT hostname) AS value FROM lamost_observations WHERE hostname IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Host Stars Covered

panels.description: Mean effective temperature of LAMOST-observed host stars derived from low-resolution spectral fitting. The Sun has Teff ≈ 5,778 K.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(teff)::numeric, 0) AS value FROM lamost_observations WHERE teff IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Mean Teff (K)

panels.description: Mean metallicity [Fe/H] of observed host stars. Negative = metal-poor; positive = metal-rich. Solar metallicity = 0.000 dex. Metal-rich stars preferentially host gas giants (planet-metallicity correlation).

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(feh)::numeric, 3) AS value FROM lamost_observations WHERE feh IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Mean [Fe/H] (dex)

panels.description: Average signal-to-noise ratio per pixel in the SDSS g-band (~4000–5500 Å). SNR > 10 is the typical minimum for reliable stellar parameter derivation from LAMOST LRS spectra.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(snr_g)::numeric, 1) AS value FROM lamost_observations WHERE snr_g IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Avg SNR g-band

panels.description: Observations with g-band SNR > 20, indicating high-confidence stellar parameter measurements suitable for precise characterisation of exoplanet host stars.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM lamost_observations WHERE snr_g > 20

panels.title: High-Quality Spectra (SNR > 20)

panels.description: LAMOST pipeline classification of each observed spectrum. STAR encompasses all stellar spectra. GALAXY and QSO indicate non-stellar matches, which may occur due to blending or mis-targeting. UNKNOWN are spectra the pipeline could not classify.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE spec_class = 'STAR') AS "Star", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE spec_class = 'GALAXY') AS "Galaxy", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE spec_class = 'QSO') AS "QSO", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE spec_class = 'UNKNOWN' OR spec_class IS NULL) AS "Unknown" FROM lamost_observations

panels.title: Spectral Class Distribution

panels.description: Top 15 most frequent spectral subclasses from LAMOST stellar spectra (e.g. G2, K5, F9). Filtered to stellar spectra with a non-empty subclass only.

targets.rawSql: SELECT spec_subclass AS "Subclass", COUNT(*) AS "Observations" FROM lamost_observations WHERE spec_subclass IS NOT NULL AND spec_subclass != '' AND spec_class = 'STAR' GROUP BY spec_subclass ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 15

panels.title: Top 15 Spectral Subclasses

panels.description: Distribution of host star effective temperatures from LAMOST spectral fits, grouped by spectral class. The bulk of LAMOST exoplanet host targets are FGK dwarfs in the 4000–7000 K range.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN teff < 3700 THEN '1. M (<3700 K)' WHEN teff < 5200 THEN '2. K (3700-5200 K)' WHEN teff < 6000 THEN '3. G (5200-6000 K)' WHEN teff < 7500 THEN '4. F (6000-7500 K)' ELSE '5. A+ (>7500 K)' END AS "Spectral Type", COUNT(*) AS "Observations" FROM lamost_observations WHERE teff IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Teff Distribution by Spectral Type

panels.description: Metallicity distribution of LAMOST-observed host stars. Metal-rich stars ([Fe/H] > 0) are statistically more likely to host gas giants — the planet-metallicity correlation. Sub-solar metallicity is typical of older Galactic disk stars.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN feh < -1.0 THEN '1. < -1.0 (Metal-poor)' WHEN feh < -0.5 THEN '2. -1.0 to -0.5' WHEN feh < 0.0 THEN '3. -0.5 to 0.0' WHEN feh < 0.5 THEN '4. 0.0 to +0.5' ELSE '5. > +0.5 (Metal-rich)' END AS "Metallicity", COUNT(*) AS "Observations" FROM lamost_observations WHERE feh IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: [Fe/H] Metallicity Distribution

panels.description: Surface gravity (log g) distribution. Main-sequence dwarfs cluster at log g ≈4.0–4.5 (Sun: log g = 4.44 cgs). Subgiants fall near 3.0–4.0; evolved giants have log g < 3.0. Compact objects would appear above 5.0.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN logg < 2.0 THEN '1. < 2.0 (Giant)' WHEN logg < 3.0 THEN '2. 2.0-3.0 (Subgiant)' WHEN logg < 4.0 THEN '3. 3.0-4.0 (Evolved)' WHEN logg < 5.0 THEN '4. 4.0-5.0 (Main Seq)' ELSE '5. > 5.0 (Compact)' END AS "log g bin", COUNT(*) AS "Observations" FROM lamost_observations WHERE logg IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Surface Gravity (log g) Distribution

panels.description: Heliocentric radial velocity (HRV) distribution. The spread reflects Milky Way disk kinematics. Stars near 0 km/s co-move with the Sun; large |HRV| values may indicate thick disk or halo membership, or unresolved binaries.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN hrv < -100 THEN '1. < -100 km/s' WHEN hrv < -50 THEN '2. -100 to -50' WHEN hrv < 0 THEN '3. -50 to 0' WHEN hrv < 50 THEN '4. 0 to +50' WHEN hrv < 100 THEN '5. +50 to +100' ELSE '6. > +100 km/s' END AS "HRV Range", COUNT(*) AS "Observations" FROM lamost_observations WHERE hrv IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Heliocentric Radial Velocity Distribution

panels.description: Top 50 LAMOST observations ranked by g-band SNR. All columns are sortable and filterable. Teff, log g, and [Fe/H] are LAMOST spectroscopic measurements; HRV is the heliocentric radial velocity in km/s.

targets.rawSql: SELECT hostname AS "Host Star", COALESCE(spec_subclass, '?') AS "Subclass", ROUND(teff::numeric, 0) AS "Teff (K)", ROUND(e_teff::numeric, 0) AS "±Teff", ROUND(logg::numeric, 2) AS "log g", ROUND(e_logg::numeric, 2) AS "±log g", ROUND(feh::numeric, 3) AS "[Fe/H]", ROUND(hrv::numeric, 1) AS "HRV (km/s)", ROUND(snr_g::numeric, 1) AS "SNR g", ROUND(snr_r::numeric, 1) AS "SNR r", obs_date AS "Obs Date", obsid AS "ObsID" FROM lamost_observations WHERE snr_g IS NOT NULL ORDER BY snr_g DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: Best LAMOST Observations (by SNR)

options.content:
### Exoplanet Catalog Context
The panels below draw from the full **NASA Exoplanet Archive** (sourced daily via TAP). The final table shows confirmed exoplanets whose host stars fall within LAMOST’s observable range (V mag 10–17.8, dec > −10°) — stars where cross-matching with LAMOST DR5 may be possible.

panels.description: Total confirmed exoplanets in the NASA Exoplanet Archive as of the last daily ingest.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM exoplanets

panels.title: Total Confirmed Exoplanets

panels.description: Planets with equilibrium temperature 180–310 K, placing them in the conservative habitable zone.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE is_potentially_habitable = true

panels.title: Habitable Zone Candidates

panels.description: Host stars with Teff 5200–6000 K, the G spectral class (same as our Sun at 5,778 K).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT hostname) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE st_teff BETWEEN 5200 AND 6000

panels.title: Sun-like Host Stars (G-type)

panels.description: Host systems with two or more confirmed planets, indicating dynamically stable multi-body architectures.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT hostname) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_pnum >= 2

panels.title: Multi-Planet Systems

panels.description: Distance to the nearest confirmed exoplanet host in light-years (1 pc = 3.26 ly).

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND((MIN(sy_dist) * 3.26156)::numeric, 2) AS value FROM exoplanets

panels.title: Nearest Exoplanet (ly)

panels.description: Confirmed exoplanets whose host star falls within LAMOST’s observable magnitude window (V mag 10–17.8) and northern sky footprint (dec > −10°), making spectroscopic cross-matching with LAMOST DR5 feasible.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_vmag BETWEEN 10 AND 17.8 AND dec > -10

panels.title: Planets in LAMOST Range

panels.description: Detection method breakdown across the full NASA exoplanet catalog. Transit dominates due to Kepler and TESS. The LAMOST-observable subset is skewed toward Radial Velocity targets, which tend to be brighter host stars.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE discoverymethod = 'Transit') AS "Transit", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE discoverymethod = 'Radial Velocity') AS "Radial Velocity", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE discoverymethod = 'Microlensing') AS "Microlensing", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE discoverymethod = 'Imaging') AS "Imaging", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE discoverymethod = 'Transit Timing Variations') AS "Transit Timing Variations", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE discoverymethod NOT IN ('Transit','Radial Velocity','Microlensing','Imaging','Transit Timing Variations')) AS "Other" FROM exoplanets WHERE discoverymethod IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Discovery Methods (Full Catalog)

panels.description: Planet size-class distribution across the full NASA catalog. Rocky < 1.5 R⊕ | Super-Earth 1.5–2.5 | Sub-Neptune 2.5–4 | Neptune-like 4–10 | Gas Giant > 10. LAMOST-observed hosts tend to have a higher fraction of gas giants due to the planet-metallicity correlation.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pl_rade < 1.5) AS "Rocky (<1.5 R⊕)", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pl_rade >= 1.5 AND pl_rade < 2.5) AS "Super-Earth (1.5-2.5)", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pl_rade >= 2.5 AND pl_rade < 4.0) AS "Sub-Neptune (2.5-4)", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pl_rade >= 4.0 AND pl_rade < 10.0) AS "Neptune-like (4-10)", COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pl_rade >= 10.0) AS "Gas Giant (>10)" FROM exoplanets WHERE pl_rade IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Planet Size Classes (Full Catalog)

panels.description: Confirmed exoplanets whose host stars lie within LAMOST’s photometric range (V mag 10–17.8) and sky footprint (dec > −10°), sorted by V magnitude. These are the best candidates for cross-referencing with LAMOST spectroscopic data.

targets.rawSql: SELECT hostname AS "Host Star", pl_name AS "Planet", ROUND(sy_vmag::numeric, 2) AS "V Mag", ROUND((sy_dist * 3.26156)::numeric, 1) AS "Dist (ly)", discoverymethod AS "Method", disc_year AS "Year", ROUND(st_teff::numeric, 0) AS "Teff (K)", ROUND(st_met::numeric, 3) AS "[Fe/H]", ROUND(pl_rade::numeric, 2) AS "R⊕", ROUND(pl_eqt::numeric, 0) AS "Eq Temp (K)", CASE WHEN is_potentially_habitable THEN '✓ HZ' ELSE '' END AS "HZ" FROM exoplanets WHERE sy_vmag BETWEEN 10 AND 17.8 AND dec > -10 ORDER BY sy_vmag ASC LIMIT 50

panels.title: Exoplanets in LAMOST Observable Range
